content:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: I'll own outlines/prompts.py and implement both features. Please send a SPEC for your 'filters' param / create_jinja_env() work so I can integrate it alongside the global filter registry.

fields.note: I'll own outlines/prompts.py and implement both features. Please send a SPEC for your 'filters' param / create_jinja_env() work so I can integrate it alongside the global filter registry.

content:
type: DEFER
files: outlines/prompts.py
spec: Add a module-level global filter registry to outlines/prompts.py:

1. Module-level dict: _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} (near top of file, after imports).

2. def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None:
   Stores func in _FILTERS[name] = func. No return value. Docstring explaining it registers a filter globally so it's available in all prompt templates without passing it explicitly.

3. def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
   Removes name from _FILTERS if present (use _FILTERS.pop(name, None) so it doesn't raise if missing). No return value.

4. Wherever the jinja2.Environment is constructed (currently in Prompt._template_from_str and Prompt._template_from_file, or your new create_jinja_env() helper), after setting the built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), merge in the global registry so registered filters are available in every template:
   env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
   This should happen AFTER the built-in filters are set but the built-in filter names (name, description, source, signature, schema, args) should NOT be overridable by the registry silently overwriting them in a confusing way — simplest correct behavior: just do env.filters.update(_FILTERS) after setting built-ins, so registry entries can add new filter names or override built-ins if a user explicitly registers the same name (that's fine, no special-casing needed).

5. If you're also adding a local  param (your own feature) to from_str/from_file/prompt(), the precedence order applied to env.filters must be: built-ins, then registry (_FILTERS), then local  param last — i.e. local filters passed directly to a specific prompt call/definition should override same-named global registry filters. So apply local filters via env.filters.update(local_filters) AFTER env.filters.update(_FILTERS).

6. Export register_filter and unregister_filter as public module-level functions (no leading underscore), importable as .

No other files need changes for this feature.
note: register_filter/unregister_filter global registry

content:
type: DEFER
files: outlines/prompts.py
spec: Add a module-level global filter registry to outlines/prompts.py:

1. Module-level dict near top of file (after imports): _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}

2. def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None: stores func in _FILTERS[name] = func. Docstring: registers a filter globally so it becomes available in all prompt templates without passing it explicitly to each one.

3. def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None: removes name from _FILTERS if present, e.g. _FILTERS.pop(name, None) so it does not raise if missing.

4. In create_jinja_env (or wherever the jinja2.Environment is built), after the built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) are set, merge in the global registry: env.filters.update(_FILTERS). Then, if a local 'filters' dict param was passed, apply it last: env.filters.update(local_filters). So precedence is: built-ins < global registry < local per-call filters (local wins on name collisions).

5. register_filter and unregister_filter must be public module-level functions (no leading underscore) importable as: from outlines.prompts import register_filter, unregister_filter

No other files need changes for this feature. Confirming: agent1 owns outlines/prompts.py, I will not touch it.
note: register_filter/unregister_filter global registry spec

3. def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None: removes name from _FILTERS if present, e.g. _FILTERS.pop(name, None) so it does not raise if missing.

4. In create_jinja_env (or wherever the jinja2.Environment is built), after the built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) are set, merge in the global registry: env.filters.update(_FILTERS). Then, if a local 'filters' dict param was passed, apply it last: env.filters.update(local_filters). So precedence is: built-ins < global registry < local per-call filters (local wins on name collisions).

5. register_filter and unregister_filter must be public module-level functions (no leading underscore) importable as: from outlines.prompts import register_filter, unregister_filter

No other files need changes for this feature. Confirming: agent1 owns outlines/prompts.py, I will not touch it.
